## Summary
<!-- One paragraph describing what this PR does and why. -->

## Spec reference
<!-- Use an exact path like docs/specs/active/agent-runtime-hardening-phase-1.md. For small fixes, use [no-spec] in the PR title and set this line to [no-spec]. -->
Spec: docs/specs/active/<slug>.md

## Type of change
- [ ] Greenfield feature (new functionality)
- [ ] Brownfield improvement (existing code quality)
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Dependency update
- [ ] Infrastructure / CI change

## Checklist
- [ ] Spec reference is a specific active spec path, or `[no-spec]` is justified in the PR title
- [ ] All acceptance criteria in the spec are checked off
- [ ] Unit tests added and passing
- [ ] Integration tests added and passing
- [ ] E2E test added (if user-facing change)
- [ ] Regression risk assessed and covered where existing behavior changed
- [ ] Reviewer agent run and all Critical/High issues resolved
- [ ] CI is green (lint, typecheck, tests, coverage, build)
- [ ] No secrets or PII introduced
- [ ] Observability added (logs, metrics) for new services or endpoints
- [ ] Accessibility requirements met (if UI change)
- [ ] Documentation updated for workflow, setup, architecture, or contributor-facing behavior changes
- [ ] External research brief added or updated when outside references materially changed the decision

## Reviewer notes
<!-- Anything the reviewer should know: tricky areas, known trade-offs, follow-up work. -->
